actionable_feedback: Fix grader metadata contradictions: ensure 'passed_criteria' and 'failed_criteria' lists and every rubric_item_scores 'passed' boolean are consistent across all duplicated sections of the grader output.

Include workbook-level traceability: either (a) ship the workbook with visible formulas (no broken external references) or (b) provide an accompanying reproducible script (Python/CSV) that computes the same cells from the packet fields.

Add explicit cross-reference mapping in the owner memo: for each memo claim (e.g., R4 payback = 24.21) list the workbook sheet name and exact cell address (or script file and line) that produces that value.

Show payback derivation explicitly: include the retrofit cost and annual savings line items used and the formula (payback = retrofit_cost / annual_savings), and point to the cells or script lines that compute those values.
Annotate which cells are hard-coded versus formula-driven in the workbook and, if any inputs are assumed, document them in the memo.
Run an environment compatibility check (or provide a plain-text exported computation sheet) so automated verifiers can open and inspect formulas (resolve the openpyxl / preview limitation).
feedback_summary: The submission includes the required artifacts and the owner memo clearly reports validation outcomes and numeric claims (baseline payback 44.76, best 24.21, improvement 45.91%, sensitivity 22.9995). Deterministic artifact-contract checks passed. Two substantive issues prevent full credit: (1) metadata inconsistency in grader output (ri_1 is listed both as failed and passed in different places), causing verifier to flag the grader JSON as internally contradictory; (2) workbook-level traceability could not be programmatically verified because the workbook preview failed to open (openpyxl unavailable), and the memo lacks explicit sheet/cell cross-references or a reproducibility script showing formulas/derivations (notably payback derivation from cost and annual savings). Addressing these will remove ambiguity and restore full semantic traceability.

recommended_next_steps: Provide workbook formulas or a reproducible script and cross-reference table
extra_model_fields.verifier_issues_summary: Two substantive issues: (1) grader metadata internal contradictions for ri_1 (both passed and failed flagged), (2) workbook inspection limited (openpyxl unavailable) and missing memo-to-workbook cross-references.
